10-library/15-hooks.md.edits: Header: bumped Last scan (27c8471 -> f9d7d14). usePersistedAttr section: removed inaccurate 'Sanitises stale storage values back to defaultValue on mount' line (the hook never validated values; sanitisation lives in DocsPreferencesMenu post-mount effects). Added new 'Lazy init (no-flash invariant)' subsection documenting the useState factory that reads localStorage synchronously on client mount, the SSR fallback to defaultValue, the rationale (without lazy init the post-paint DOM-write effect would clobber a host-side FOUC script), and a hard rule against reverting to useState(defaultValue). Added explicit note about argument validation regexes (SAFE_DATA_ATTR / SAFE_STORAGE_KEY) and where per-axis allow-list sanitisation lives (DocsPreferencesMenu). Cross-referenced 20-web-app/00-architecture.md for the FOUC pattern.

20-web-app/00-architecture.md.edits: Header: bumped Last scan (1637629 -> f9d7d14). Root layout JSX block updated: added the PREFERENCES_INIT_SCRIPT module-scope const and the inline <script dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: PREFERENCES_INIT_SCRIPT }}> render before the font preconnects in <head>. 'Four things load-bearing' renamed to 'Five things load-bearing': inserted item 2 documenting the FOUC script (purpose, allow-list duplication with DocsPreferencesMenu, hard constraints — synchronous, before stylesheets, hard-coded literal — and CSP note about needing 'unsafe-inline' or sha256 hash). Item 3 (formerly 2, suppressHydrationWarning) rewritten to explain the script + lazy-init usePersistedAttr combo (cross-referenced to 10-library/15-hooks.md). Items 4 (fonts) and 5 (locale) renumbered, content unchanged.

20-web-app/10-docs-site/README.md.edits: Header sha bump only (1637629 -> f9d7d14). No narrative changes.
20-web-app/10-docs-site/README.md.decision_no_chapter_edit: PR #99 rewrote the FOUC_FIX inline-string snippet inside foundations/theming/page.tsx (App Router pattern + allow-list validation, replacing a deprecated next/script beforeInteractive variant). The docs-site chapter documents page structure / Code helper props / nav contract — it does not enumerate inline snippet bodies. Treating this as the same precedent as the gh-95 'mechanical hex updates in cyberpunk example snippets' decision: SHA bump only, no chapter edit. The actual FOUC script narrative lives in 20-web-app/00-architecture.md where it belongs (it's a layout concern, not a docs-site concern).

20-web-app/20-theme-generator/README.md.edits: Header: bumped Last scan (34dbd0a -> 6a6c72e). presets.ts code block: PRESET_LABELS updated to 'Cyberpunk (violet + cyan)' and 'Forest (green + copper)' to match the rebalanced ramps (gh-95 also fixed these labels in en.json + it.json). 'Per-preset semantic overrides (gh-93)' subsection: rewritten — the override map now contains only the sunset entry; added an explanatory paragraph documenting why the cyberpunk override was retired (deep teal #0f766e contrasts AA with white, no override needed). The 'value derived from the palette' design rule and the SYNC_PRESET fix narrative remain (still load-bearing for sunset and any future override). theme.css block list: removed the line for `:root[data-palette='cyberpunk']:not([data-theme='dark'])` since gh-95 deleted that block. Added a new subsection 'Paper divergence — gh-95 precedent' with a per-preset paper.50/brand.500 table documenting that cyberpunk + forest now ship divergent paper ramps (cool cyan cream and sage cream respectively) and noting the regression guard in presets.test.ts.
20-web-app/20-theme-generator/README.md.decision_no_chapter_edit: 20-web-app/10-docs-site/README.md was again skipped (mechanical hex updates in the cyberpunk example snippets in apps/web/app/docs/foundations/{theming,colors}/page.tsx — no documented information shifts).

20-web-app/20-theme-generator/README.md.edits: Header: bumped Last scan (a9faba7 -> 34dbd0a). Summary: rewrote the preset list from 'default plum+gold / neon / sunset' to 'default plum+gold / sunset / cyberpunk / forest' to match the post-#93 roster. presets.ts section: replaced the 3-preset PresetName + PRESET_LABELS code block with the 4-preset version; added a new sub-section 'Per-preset semantic overrides (gh-93)' describing the PresetSemanticOverrides type, the PRESET_SEMANTIC_OVERRIDES map (sunset.dark.memphisBorderColor=#000000 to keep terracotta dark surfaces with a black memphis frame; cyberpunk.light.primaryForeground=ink.900 because the vivid amber brand.500=#ffab00 fails WCAG AA against white) and the new computePresetSemantic helper exported from presets.ts. Added a paragraph documenting the SYNC_PRESET regression fix: the navbar-driven path was bypassing the override merge by calling computeSemanticLight/Dark directly; both branches now route through computePresetSemantic. Cross-reference to the new e2e regression spec at e2e/tests/scenarios/palette-refresh-r2.spec.ts. computeSemanticLight/Dark section: added an opening note that presets may further override the canonical result via PRESET_SEMANTIC_OVERRIDES before the value reaches the consumer; the helpers themselves remain pure derivations of the raw palette. theme.css cross-reference paragraph: updated to mention the new block stack (sunset / cyberpunk / forest / sunset-dark / cyberpunk-light) and the source-order specificity rule called out in the file's header comment after #93.

runs.decision_no_chapter_edit: 20-web-app/10-docs-site/README.md was considered for the docs/i18n/getting-started snippet migrations (neon -> cyberpunk in code examples) but the docs-site chapter currently documents the route layout and patterns, not the content of individual code snippets. The substitutions are mechanical (the chapter would not gain or lose information). Skipped to avoid noise. 10-library/10-components/README.md was also considered for the dropdown-menu/attr-toggle-group stories.tsx changes, but those are storybook-only fixtures that the components chapter already does not document. Skipped.

20-web-app/20-theme-generator/README.md.edits: Header: bumped Last scan (e53c5be -> a9faba7); Sources extended with the new `apps/web/app/styles/__tests__/theme-css-dark-block.test.ts` source-contract guard. IdentityTheme section: added a paragraph documenting that DEFAULT_THEME's identity now diverges per-mode since #91 — `DEFAULT_IDENTITY_LIGHT` vs `DEFAULT_IDENTITY_DARK` — with the Plum+Gold dark deltas listed (medals.gold/master.outer = paper.50; charts.{1..5} adapted to ink.300/brand.400/dark-success/dark-destructive/ink.100; appPattern.color1/2/3 adapted; bronze/silver/grandmaster + navOnDark unchanged). presets.ts section: clarified that applyPreset preserves identity per-mode by design and added the post-#91 consequence for the RESET path (a fresh DEFAULT_THEME load picks up the new dark identity; an already-diverged user keeps customisations across preset switches; no in-app affordance for partial dark-only reset). computeSemanticLight/Dark section: replaced the brief 'inverts surfaces and adjusts status' summary with the full enumeration of #91 dark deltas — muted=ink.800 (collapses card+muted in dark, intentional), mutedForeground=paper.50 (full-white-on-muted by user choice, light keeps ink.700), primary=brand.400 (Memphis legibility on dark plum), ring/badgeFeatured following primary, warning=#e8a435 literal (decoupled from primary), memphisBorderColor=#cccccc (visible 2px frame on dark surfaces, while memphisShadowColor stays #000000 to match the per-tier foundation tokens). Cross-reference added to the new theme-css-dark-block source-contract test. exporters.ts delta-encoding section: noted that with the post-#91 identity divergence, the dark block always emits identity-delta lines even when `semanticDark: false` is set (pre-#91 they were a shared reference and produced an empty dark block); cited exporters.test.ts coverage of both branches.

runs.decision_no_chapter_edit: Originally queued under both `10-library/20-theming/README.md` and `20-web-app/20-theme-generator/README.md`. The theming chapter sources are 100% `packages/ui/...`; #91 only touched `apps/web/...` (docs site + generator). The lib's tokens.css ships neutral grayscale only, the lib's theme.css is unchanged, so the theming chapter has nothing to update. The theme-generator chapter owns `apps/web/app/styles/theme.css` + `apps/web/app/theme-generator/theme-state.ts`, which carry both halves of #91, so it absorbs the entire update.

30-cross-cutting/15-e2e.md.edits: Header bump (last_scan e53c5be -> 08ede58). Layout tree: added favicon-suite.spec.ts (after issue-fixes) and tinted-memphis-shadow-runtime.spec.ts (after comprehensive-audit). Configuration: rewrote `webServer` paragraph as a CI-aware bullet list — local boots `next dev` with `reuseExistingServer: true`; CI boots `next start` against a freshly-built `.next` (PR #90), `reuseExistingServer: false`. Smoke and docs flows: added `favicon-suite.spec.ts` bullet describing the Next 14 metadata link emission + asset 200/MIME contract (PR #78). Theme-generator runtime audits table: added `tinted-memphis-shadow-runtime.spec.ts` row (regression guard for #66 — per-color @utility tinted Memphis shadows, intent-token override at runtime). Cross-browser stabilization patterns: added helper #6 — `<style>`-tag injection over inline `setProperty(...,'important')` for `@utility`-resolved custom properties (PR #66 / c4aa1ba). Running in CI: rewrote with the four PR #90 optimizations — Playwright browser cache (~/.cache/ms-playwright keyed on pnpm-lock.yaml hash; install step skipped on cache hit; system deps always install), lib build artifact reuse (damo-ui-dist between jobs), prod webserver (next start vs next dev), concurrency cancellation with `head_ref || ref` fallback (load-bearing for push+PR same-SHA dedup). Notes & gotchas #5 added: webkit-only flakes are quarantined via `test.skip(({ browserName }) => ...)` with linked issues, not patched in place (b11ce4c, 4bd889f, c4aa1ba).

runs.decision_defer_10_ci_cd: Queue trigger also touched .github/workflows/ci.yml, which would map to chapter 30-cross-cutting/10-ci-cd.md. That chapter is status:pending (never documented). Per Kipi rules, *4 updates documented chapters; first-scan work is a *2 task. Deferred 10-ci-cd.md to a dedicated *2 run that should cover both ci.yml and publish.yml end-to-end rather than starting from a thin patch.

20-web-app/00-architecture.md.edits: Header bump (last_scan 27c8471 -> d0f212a). Sources extended with `apps/web/public/{icons/source/favicon-source.svg,site.webmanifest,favicon*,apple-touch-icon.png,android-chrome-*.png}` and `apps/web/scripts/generate-icons.ts`. Top-level layout tree: replaced 'static assets (mascot images)' line with full `public/` listing + new `scripts/` block. Root layout section: prepended a `metadata` (icons + manifest + metadataBase) and `viewport` (themeColor) export block ahead of the JSX, with prose noting Next 14's themeColor split. Added new section 'Branding assets — favicon pipeline' between Top-level shared components and Notes & gotchas: documents the source SVG (Memphis 'D' purple/gold/black contract), the deterministic sharp + inline-ICO pipeline (per-target density cap, MSDN-compliant ICONDIRENTRY), the rationale for inlining the encoder vs `to-ico` (CVE chain), and the rationale for committing generated PNGs vs regenerating on every build.

runs.decision_no_new_chapter: Spec floated `20-web-app/40-branding-assets.md` as a possible new chapter. Folded under 00-architecture instead: the favicon pipeline is ~150 LOC and the manifest is 30 lines of JSON — no precedent for branding-only sub-chapters in the existing layout. Adding one would create a tiny chapter that mostly duplicates the Root layout's metadata wiring.

sha bump only — chapter is high-level wiring/i18n/tailwind, doesn't deep-document landing page sections. The hero swap, headline rewrite, MemphisShape removal, image col rebalance, QUICK INSTALL restructure, and i18n catalog reshape are all reflected in the underlying code without needing chapter prose changes.

Documented new Code component opt-in modes: fillHeight (flex-1 flex flex-col on wrapper, shrink-0 on header, flex-1 on content) for stretching inside flex parents — used by the home QUICK INSTALL card grid. Also documented the slim GitHub-style scrollbar styling (Tailwind arbitrary variants for ::-webkit-scrollbar + scrollbar-width: thin) applied to every Code block.
